Title: Total biosynthesis of opiates by stepwise fermentation using engineered Escherichia coli

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Fusion protein containing cytochrome P450 domain (CYP80Y2) and oxidoreductase domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts (S)-reticuline toward (R)-reticuline but showed very low functional activity in E. coli; not practical under current conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; N-terminal truncation; 5-ALA supplementation; LC-MS chiral analysis of reticuline; SDS-PAGE
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STORR was identified as the enzyme converting (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline. 2. N-terminal truncation improved expression in E. coli, and 5-ALA addition enabled detectable activity. 3. Product formation remained below 10 μM, so STORR was considered unsuitable for practical opiate production in the current E. coli system.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG; N-terminally truncated form expressed better
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated P450; heterologously expressed in E. coli
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-aminolevulinic acid supplementation required for detectable activity; IPTG induction
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low activity; requires expensive 5-ALA; poor practicality for production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: STORR includes a CYP80Y2 P450 domain and requires a CPR partner; full-length and N-terminal truncated forms were tested.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Served as the most suitable reductase partner for P450-dependent steps in engineered E. coli
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crude extract CPR activity assay using bovine cytochrome c; heterologous expression comparison
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATR2 displayed the strongest CPR activity among tested reductases. 2. It was chosen as the reductase partner for STORR and SalS in E. coli. 3. ATR2 supported functional P450-based opiate pathway steps in the production system.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATR2 outperformed ATR2Ncut, PsCPR and RnCPR in the authors' E. coli assay.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates tetrahydropapaveroline pathway intermediates at the amine nitrogen during reticuline formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with 4'OMT enabled production of (R)-reticuline from (R,S)-THP in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli strain AN1600; feeding with fermentatively produced (R,S)-THP; LC-MS and chiral analysis of reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNMT combined with 4'OMT was sufficient to synthesize reticuline from (R,S)-THP. 2. This two-enzyme system unexpectedly yielded detectable (R)-reticuline. 3. The resulting (R)-reticuline supported downstream thebaine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7, inducible construct but reticuline production step performed without IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered E. coli strain AN1600
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ~100 μM (R,S)-THP feeding; glucose addition; IPTG inhibits reticuline production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely stereochemical bias favoring S-form reticuline, lowering effective R-form yield
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used instead of the full three-methyltransferase combination because the latter produced only S-reticuline under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; (R)-reticuline productivity identified as major bottleneck overall

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates THP pathway intermediates and unexpectedly also catalyses 6-O-methylation activity toward THP, enabling reticuline formation without 6OMT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A key methyltransferase enabling (R,S)-reticuline, including the R-form, from (R,S)-THP without 6OMT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crude enzyme assay with THP substrate; LC-MS/MS detection of 6-O-methyl THP, 4'-O-methyl THP, and 6,4'-O-dimethyl THP; heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4'OMT exhibited both 4'-O-methyltransferase and unexpected 6OMT-like activity toward THP. 2. This dual activity allowed reticuline production without co-expression of 6OMT. 3. Combined with CNMT, 4'OMT enabled production of usable (R)-reticuline for downstream thebaine synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7, inducible construct but reticuline production step performed without IPTG induction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in engineered E. coli strains AN1028 and AN1600
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: THP feeding; glucose addition; IPTG inhibits reticuline production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: S-form preference may reduce R-reticuline fraction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper text denotes this enzyme as 4'OMT and also as 3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine 4'-O-methyltransferase in Figure 1 abbreviation list.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enabling step for R-reticuline route in this engineered system

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Opioid analgesic precursor and raw material for opioid preparations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled functional salutaridine synthesis in E. coli and was selected for total opiate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: N-terminal truncation and heterologous expression with ATR2; SDS-PAGE; LC-MS and MS/MS identification of salutaridine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. N-terminal truncation was necessary for robust SalS expression in E. coli. 2. SalSNcut converted reticuline to salutaridine in vivo. 3. Unlike STORR, SalSNcut activity did not require 5-ALA and was suitable for practical pathway construction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG; truncated form expressed well whereas full length did not
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated P450; heterologously expressed in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Full-length SalS poorly expressed in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in downstream production strains AN1096 and AN1829; partnered with ATR2.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Strong downstream pathway enzyme; not identified as main bottleneck

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces salutaridine to salutaridinol using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Opioid analgesic precursor and raw material for opioid preparations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functioned in E. coli with SalSNcut and SalAT to enable thebaine biosynthesis from reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in AN1829; feeding authentic (R,S)-reticuline; LC-MS/MS detection of thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalR was functionally expressed in E. coli. 2. Together with SalSNcut and SalAT, it enabled conversion of reticuline to thebaine. 3. The downstream pathway from (R)-reticuline to thebaine showed high efficiency in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Requires glucose-supported NADPH generation for efficient thebaine production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion identifies SalR as part of the strong enzyme set likely underlying improved thebaine production in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not main bottleneck; downstream conversion from R-reticuline was efficient

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to salutaridinol to form salutaridinol 7-O-acetate, enabling subsequent thebaine formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Opioid analgesic precursor and raw material for opioid preparations
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required with SalSNcut and SalR for thebaine production in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in AN1829; feeding authentic (R,S)-reticuline; LC-MS/MS detection of thebaine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SalAT functioned in E. coli to support final conversion to thebaine. 2. Along with SalSNcut and SalR, it enabled detectable and efficient thebaine production from reticuline. 3. Glucose was essential, consistent with acetyl-CoA demand for the acetylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glucose required, likely to supply acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors specifically linked glucose requirement to acetyl-CoA supply for the SalAT-catalysed step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes the 6-O-methyl group from thebaine in the hydrocodone synthesis route
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Addition of this gene to thebaine-producing strain enabled hydrocodone biosynthesis in the four-step E. coli system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway extension in strain AN1942; four-step culture; LC-MS/MS detection of hydrocodone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introducing thebaine 6-O-demethylase expanded thebaine biosynthesis to hydrocodone production. 2. The engineered strain AN1942 produced hydrocodone from simple carbon source via the stepwise culture system. 3. This demonstrated flexibility of the E. coli platform for downstream opioid production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG in AN1942
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species was not specified in the provided text.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces an enone opioid intermediate to form hydrocodone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for hydrocodone production when added to the thebaine biosynthesis system
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway extension in AN1942; LC-MS/MS confirmation of hydrocodone; enzymatic preparation of hydrocodone standard from codeine using COR and MorB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MorB enabled final reduction step(s) needed for hydrocodone biosynthesis in engineered E. coli. 2. Together with thebaine 6-O-demethylase, it converted thebaine pathway output into hydrocodone. 3. Hydrocodone production confirmed modular extensibility of the platform.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in engineered E. coli after IPTG in AN1942
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MorB was also used with COR to prepare a hydrocodone standard from codeine.
